lines.text: Examples: TetA (tetracyclines), AcrAB-TolC (multi-drug, gram-negative), NorA (fluoroquinolones in Staph), MexAB-OprM (Pseudomonas).
Examples: PBP2a/mecA (MRSA), 23S rRNA mutations (macrolide, linezolid), GyrA mutations (fluoroquinolones), D-Ala-D-Lac switch (VRE).
Examples: beta-lactamases (penicillinase, TEM-1, ESBLs, KPC, NDM, OXA-48, VIM); aminoglycoside-modifying enzymes (AAC, APH, ANT); chloramphenicol acetyltransferase.
(4) Reduced permeability: keep the drug out.
Examples: porin loss (OmpF/OmpC in E. coli, OprD in Pseudomonas).

Penicillinase (1940s, Staph) → TEM-1 (1965, E. coli) → ESBLs (1980s, mutated TEM/SHV/CTX-M extending spectrum to 3rd-gen cephalosporins) → carbapenemases (KPC 1996; NDM 2008; OXA-48 2001; VIM 1999; IMP).
Enterococcus faecium, Staphylococcus aureus, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Acinetobacter baumannii, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Enterobacter species.
1.27 million deaths in 2019 directly attributable to AMR (Lancet 2022).
120,000 invasive MRSA cases/year in the US.
CRE bloodstream infection mortality up to 50%.
NDM-1 from one Swedish patient in 2008 to >70 countries by 2012.
mcr-1 first reported November 2015, found in >30 countries within months.
Transformation (uptake of naked DNA); conjugation (plasmid transfer via sex pilus, the dominant mechanism for clinical resistance); transduction (phage-mediated).
Mobile genetic elements: plasmids (especially R plasmids), transposons, integrons.
2019: WHO lists AMR in top 10 global health threats.
Resistance is evolution we watch happen in real time.
Fleming was the original Cassandra — said it clearly in 1945; we did it anyway.
Horizontal gene transfer is USB drives at a hacker conference: bacteria swap resistance genes with neighbors across species lines.
A single plasmid can carry resistance to 5+ antibiotic classes — modular cassettes, multi-drug-resistant in one transfer.
Agricultural use is the unspoken half of the problem — half of US antibiotic tonnage.
The environmental resistome is ancient — vanA homologs in 30,000-year-old permafrost.
The discovery void of 1987-2000 happened because the economics broke.
The realistic future is polypharmacy — phage, AI-driven discovery, narrow-spectrum, vaccines, microbiome restoration, stewardship — no single magic bullet.
